outcome.observable_result: MVP OS v2.1.0 is published as a backward-compatible release with executable conditional gates and synchronized downstream templates.
acceptance_criteria: Outcome-First and Reuse-First preflight rejects incomplete contracts.
Web, integration, stateful, and optional Hermes acceptance reject false or incomplete evidence.
Project Control v2 remains compatible and new source-event catalogs are traceable.
Canonical and template skills are byte-identical.
Full verification and independent reviews pass before publication.
outcome.realistic_data: Negative and positive fixtures reproduce the MaxCRM failure modes without live sends or private data.
outcome.external_result: The source branch and annotated v2.1.0 tag are pushed after verification.
outcome.cost_scale_contract: Use the Python standard library only; conditional gates add no runtime requirement to projects outside their declared scope.
outcome.time_budget: Complete one bounded release cycle and stop on verification, review, Git, or publication failure.
outcome.stop_condition: Stop before publication if any gate, source/template consistency check, Project Control check, or independent review fails.
forbidden_simplifications: Do not replace executable enforcement with documentation-only checks.
Do not modify published Project Control release 000002.
Do not claim an official release before main, tag, verification, and push agree.
outcome.rollback: Do not push the release tag on failure; if publication has not completed, keep v2.0.0 as the official release.

chain.external_signal: The MaxCRM improvement handoff identifies concrete product and process failures.
chain.interpretation: The failures are mapped to conditional MVP OS gates and canonical ownership boundaries.
chain.decision_policy: Backward compatibility and minimal dependency-free enforcement determine the v2.1 design.
chain.action: Update the skill, templates, validator, tests, decisions, release metadata, and Project Control release.
chain.external_result: The verified source release and matching tag are pushed.
chain.product_display: Bootstrap and release artifacts expose the current v2.1 architecture, exclusions, gates, and next step.
chain.independent_verification: Implementation and clean-context reviews pass against automated fixtures and the final release state.

discovery.rationale: Extend Project Control v2, the existing hard execution gate, release templates, and standard-library test harness.
The enforcement is repository-local policy validation and needs no external API or SDK.
A third-party policy engine would add dependency and migration cost beyond the bounded JSON contract.
Gate validation must remain deterministic and available without network or connector state.
The Python standard library already covers JSON parsing, validation logic, CLI handling, and tests.
A hosted service would weaken local reproducibility and add an unnecessary availability dependency.

reuse.custom_code_reason: A small repository-native validator is the minimal way to enforce the project-specific conditional contract without dependencies.
